description:
Post-finalization spec revision 2026-06-05 (bl-2e26 §4). Amends landed bl-4c54 ('EffectiveConfig: config VALUES layer down the trail', commit d59f820) — assume it landed; this corrects it.

WHY: config-shadowing down the trail and 'install' were two mechanisms for one job and contradicted on consent (install gates adoption; shadowing leaked around it). A shadowed tasks_branch could silently redirect where you write. Killed by subtraction (§15).

WHAT (src/config.rs EffectiveConfig + its doctor consumer):
- DROP the trail layer. Read layers become: CLI > XDG config.toml > landing config/balls.toml > built-in defaults. EffectiveConfig::resolve no longer takes/walks a trail; it folds [landing]+XDG only (XDG and defaults are local-trusted; never a remote/down-trail input).
- The fold that adopts a center's config now happens ONCE at install time, materialized into the landing — not lazily at read.
- ADD field 'tasks_branch' (string, default 'balls/tasks') — the one config->store indirection. DROP 'branch' (the landing branch is path-derived 'balls/config', NOT a config field; the store branch is tasks_branch).
- Keep merge semantics (scalar replace; list _prepend/_append/_ban) and id_scheme.
- Single-owner note: config's transport is destructive-replace (no merge), so the landing is path-derived per-checkout and is NEVER a sync target.
title: Greenfield §4: drop trail config-layering; read landing+XDG+defaults; add tasks_branch, drop branch
